basics.name: Anmol Mansingh
basics.label: Machine Learning Engineer
basics.summary: I like to make things simpler by breaking them down, both in and around me, mostly with data techniques and a little patience :)

work.name: Applied Biostatistics Lab, University of Michigan
work.summary: Currently developing an end-to-end ML pipeline using advanced data processing techniques to automate data extraction from PubMed papers for meta-analysis. Actively leveraging few-shot prompting and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enhance data extraction accuracy and performance.

work.name: Computational HCI Lab, University of Michigan
work.summary: Teaching at Palmer Physical Laboratory (now 302 Frist Campus Center). While not a professor at Princeton, I associated with the physics professors and continued to give lectures on campus.

work.name: Barclays
work.summary: Migrated legacy monolithic payment platform to microservices with Java and Spring Boot for over 10M customers. Deployed RESTful APIs to production on Openshift containers applying 12-factor app, TDD/BDD testing and Agile methodologies, resulting in improved scalability and maintainability. Designed and implemented data processing pipelines using Apache Kafka and Oracle SQL scripts in a cross-functional effort, processing up to about 400K customers’ payment data daily. Automated SLA breach checks using Spring Batch and Tivoli Workload Scheduler to limit manual errors, reducing processing time associated with payment settlements by 40%. Integrated changes in JavaScript and Adobe Analytics to record user behavior for a high-traffic electronic consent flow, tracking about 15k unique users daily

volunteer.summary: Lead organizer for the New York City branch of the People's Climate March, the largest climate march in history.
highlights: Awarded 'Climate Hero' award by Greenpeace for my efforts organizing the march.
Men of the year 2014 by Time magazine

achievements.title: Delegate
achievements.awarder: Ministry of Youth and Sports, Government of India
achievements.summary: The Nobel Prizes are five separate prizes that, according to Alfred Nobel's will of 1895, are awarded to 'those who, during the preceding year, have conferred the greatest benefit to humankind.'

publications.name: Zur Elektrody/namik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
